label: Basic Computer Skills
description: MS Word, Excel fundamentals, email usage

questions.text: Which function in Excel is used to add up a range of cells?
What does the AVERAGE function calculate?

options.text: The highest value in a range
The arithmetic mean of a range of values
The number of cells that contain data

questions.text: In Excel, what does the VLOOKUP function do?

options.text: Searches for a value in the first column of a table and returns a value from another column in the same row
Looks up values horizontally across a row
Validates data entry in a cell

questions.text: What is the correct syntax for an IF function in Excel?
What is the purpose of a Pivot Table in Excel?

options.text: To create a backup copy of data
To summarize, analyze, explore, and present large amounts of data
To lock cells so they cannot be edited

questions.text: How do you freeze the top row in Excel so it remains visible while scrolling?

options.text: View > Freeze Panes > Freeze Top Row

questions.text: What does Conditional Formatting allow you to do in Excel?

options.text: Apply formulas only when certain conditions are met
Automatically change the formatting of cells based on their values or specific rules
Restrict data entry to specific formats
Format cells only when the spreadsheet is printed

questions.text: In the VLOOKUP function =VLOOKUP(A2, D2:F10, 3, FALSE), what does the FALSE argument mean?

options.text: The function will return FALSE if no match is found
The lookup column is sorted in descending order
The function should search from bottom to top

questions.text: Which chart type is best suited for showing the proportion of parts to a whole?
What keyboard shortcut is used to apply the Filter feature to a data range in Excel?
Which keyboard shortcut makes selected text bold in Microsoft Word?
What is the purpose of Track Changes in Microsoft Word?

options.text: To monitor how long you spend editing a document
To record insertions, deletions, and formatting changes made to a document
To automatically save versions of the document
To track who has opened the document

questions.text: How do you insert a header or footer in a Word document?
What is Mail Merge used for in Microsoft Word?

options.text: Merging two Word documents into one
Creating personalized letters, labels, or emails by combining a template with a data source
Combining multiple email attachments into a single document

questions.text: In Word, what are Styles primarily used for?

options.text: Adding decorative borders to a page
Applying consistent formatting to headings, paragraphs, and other text elements across a document
Changing the color theme of the application

questions.text: Which tab in Word allows you to change page orientation from Portrait to Landscape?
How can you insert a table in Microsoft Word?
What does the Format Painter tool do in Word?

options.text: Changes the background color of the page
Copies formatting from one piece of text and applies it to another
Paints a border around selected text
Inserts clip art into the document

questions.text: Which shortcut selects all text in a Word document?
What happens when you press Ctrl + Z in Word?

options.text: Opens the Find and Replace dialog

questions.text: What is the difference between CC and BCC in an email?

options.text: CC sends a copy; BCC sends the original
CC recipients are visible to all; BCC recipients are hidden from other recipients
CC is for internal emails; BCC is for external emails
There is no difference; they function identically

questions.text: When should you use 'Reply All' instead of 'Reply'?

options.text: Always, to make sure everyone is informed
Only when your response is relevant to all recipients in the conversation
Never, as it is considered bad email etiquette
Only when replying to your supervisor

questions.text: What is the maximum recommended file size for email attachments in most professional environments?
Which of the following is the most professional email greeting for a formal business communication?
What is an email signature?

options.text: A digital certificate that encrypts your email
A block of text automatically appended to the end of emails containing contact information and other details
A handwritten signature scanned and attached to every email
The password used to log into your email account

questions.text: What is the best practice when you receive a suspicious email with an attachment from an unknown sender?

options.text: Open the attachment to see what it contains
Forward it to all colleagues as a warning
Do not open the attachment; report it as spam or phishing and delete it
Reply to the sender asking for more information

questions.text: What are email folders and labels primarily used for?

options.text: To organize and categorize emails for easier retrieval
To compress emails and save storage space

questions.text: When composing a professional email, what should the subject line contain?

options.text: Your full name and contact details
A clear, concise description of the email's purpose
The word 'URGENT' to ensure it gets read
It should be left blank for formal emails

questions.text: What does the 'Forwarding' feature in email allow you to do?

options.text: Send a received email to a different recipient
Schedule an email to be sent at a future time
Move an email to a different folder

questions.text: If you need to send a large file (over 25 MB) to a colleague via email, what is the best approach?

options.text: Split the file into multiple smaller emails
Upload the file to a cloud storage service and share the link
Compress the file into a ZIP, which always makes it small enough
Print the file and deliver it physically

questions.text: What does the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + C do on most computers?

options.text: Copies the selected item to the clipboard

questions.text: What is the purpose of a file extension (e.g., .docx, .pdf, .xlsx)?

options.text: To indicate the file type and which application should open it
To make the file compatible with all operating systems

questions.text: What is cloud storage?

options.text: A type of USB flash drive
Storing files on remote servers accessed via the internet, such as Google Drive or OneDrive

questions.text: Which of the following is the strongest password?
What does 'phishing' refer to in computer security?

options.text: A fraudulent attempt to obtain sensitive information by disguising as a trustworthy source
A technique for speeding up internet connections

questions.text: What keyboard shortcut is used to print a document in most applications?
What is the function of the Recycle Bin (Windows) or Trash (Mac)?

options.text: It temporarily stores deleted files so they can be recovered before permanent deletion
It compresses files to save disk space

questions.text: What does the keyboard shortcut Alt + Tab do on Windows?

options.text: Switches between open applications or windows

questions.text: What does PDF stand for?
What is two-factor authentication (2FA)?

options.text: Using two different passwords for the same account
A security method that requires two different forms of verification to access an account, such as a password and a code sent to your phone
Logging into two accounts at the same time
Having two people approve a login attempt
